comment.body: Now that we've heard Trump's position, it's worth remembering that Alma in her weekly post wrote extensively about the EU's and (RoW) retaliation, and that its potential severity was not reflected in the left tail.  She did not spend her valuable time writing these many paragraphs about the EU's options for no reason.  To use an American baseball analogy, Alma is batting 1000 on macro calls.  If the retaliation is severe, and it is not priced in, and it will will remain unknown until the overnight session at the earliest (and perhaps for another day or two even), the sell probably isn't over.  Also, Alma also is very careful,  precise, and objective with language.  The words "a black swan scenario around tariffs" did not type themselves into her weekly post.  Last, she's repeatedly and consistently referenced the bottom around last April's levels.  The setup for this outcome seems to be playing out.

comment.body:
The descent down from here - I will be interested to know what Alma thinks the movement will be. 

Is this a spot decay/vol down type of drop like we had in late February - where it was a controlled drawdown and the skew favored being long calls on brief reversions back up, or will we see vol expansion?
